title: Linzer biscuits
subtitle: Austrian almond sandwich biscuits with a jam-filled cut-out window
excerpt: Spiced almond shortbread biscuits cut in pairs, with a small round or star cut from one half so the jam filling shows through. A miniature Linzer Torte in biscuit form, a Viennese coffeehouse classic.
sourceNotes: Linzer biscuits derive from the Linzer Torte, an Austrian almond tart with lattice pastry and raspberry jam filling that is one of the oldest documented cake recipes (the Linzer Torte recipe appears in a 1653 Austrian manuscript). The biscuit form (Linzer Augen, Linzer cookies) is a domestic miniaturisation of the tart documented in Austro-Hungarian cookery from the nineteenth century. Conrad Hagger, Neues Saltzburgisches Koch-Buch (1719), documents the Linzer Torte form. The biscuits are particularly associated with the Christmas season in German-speaking countries.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unbaked cut biscuits between sheets of baking paper. Bake from frozen, adding 2-3 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The dough keeps refrigerated for 3 days before rolling and cutting.

content.text: Linzer biscuits are cut in matched pairs: one plain round or heart, one with a small hole cut from the centre. The two halves are baked together, sandwiched with jam (the jam visible through the window), and dusted with icing sugar. The dough is an almond and spice shortbread — slightly more fragile than plain shortbread because of the ground almonds. It must be well chilled before rolling or it will stick and tear.

Mix together the {{plain-flour}}, {{ground-almonds}}, {{icing-sugar}}, {{ground-cinnamon}}, and {{ground-cloves}}. Rub in the cold {{unsalted-butter}} until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Add the {{whole-eggs}} and {{lemon-zest}} and bring together into a smooth dough. Press into a disc and chill for at least 1 hour.

Heat the oven to 180°C (160°C fan). Roll the dough out on a lightly floured surface to about 4 mm thick. Cut out an even number of 6-7 cm rounds (or hearts). Cut a small hole (2 cm round, or a star) from the centre of half the rounds: these are the tops. Place all the biscuits on lined baking trays and chill for 20 minutes. Bake for 10-12 minutes until pale golden at the edges. Cool completely on the tray.

Dust the top biscuits (the ones with the holes) with icing sugar. Spread a teaspoon of {{raspberry-jam}} on each plain base, spreading to within 5 mm of the edge. Press the dusted tops gently onto the jam-covered bases. Leave to set for 30 minutes before serving.
